title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Constipation
intro_desc: When it comes to constipation, herbal essential oils can be a great help. These oils are extracted from plants and can be used in various ways, such as adding a few drops to your bath water or mixing them with a carrier oil for a massage. Some herbs are particularly good at relieving constipation, like Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel. Fennel seeds have a natural laxative effect, which helps to stimulate digestion and move food through the digestive system. This can be really beneficial for people who struggle with constipation, as it can help to prevent discomfort and pain.  Another herb that's often used to relieve constipation is Zingiber officinale, or ginger. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation. This can help to ease any discomfort or pain caused by constipation, and also reduce the risk of more serious health problems.  Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, is another herb that's often used to relieve constipation. Licorice root has a natural anti-inflammatory effect, which can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation. It can also help to stimulate digestion and improve bowel movements, which can help to relieve constipation.  Using herbal essential oils for constipation can bring many benefits to your life. Not only can they help to relieve constipation, but they can also help to improve digestion and reduce the risk of more serious health problems. Additionally, many herbal essential oils have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and soothe the digestive system. This can be really beneficial for people who struggle with digestive issues, as it can help to improve their overall health and wellbeing.  Herbal teas can also be really helpful in relieving constipation. These teas are made by infusing herbs in hot water, which releases their natural oils and properties. Drinking herbal teas can help to stimulate digestion and improve bowel movements, which can help to relieve constipation. Many herbal teas, such as fennel tea and ginger tea, are specifically designed to relieve constipation and improve digestion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains a compound called anethole, which is responsible for its carminative and spasmolytic properties. Anethole helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can cause constipation. Foeniculum vulgare essential oils also contains another compound called limonene, which has a gentle laxative effect and helps to stimulate bowel movements. The antispasmodic properties of anethole and the gentle laxative properties of limonene work together to help alleviate constipation and promote regular bowel movements. Regular use of Foeniculum vulgare essential oils can help to relieve symptoms of constipation and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for constipation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Fill a clean glass jar with 1/4 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds and cover with 1 cup of carrier oil.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container. Discard the seeds.
Bottle the Foeniculum vulgare essential oil and use 5-10 drops per day to help with constipation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains the bioactive constituents gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and improve blood flow to the intestines. The warming properties of gingerols and shogaols stimulate digestion and help to move food through the digestive system, which can help to alleviate constipation. Additionally, the essential oils have a gentle laxative effect, helping to soften and move stool through the intestines. By promoting digestive health and relieving spasms in the intestines, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help to alleviate the symptoms of constipation.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for constipation
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: 100g Zingiber officinale rhizomes, 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil), 3 tablespoons of vodka (optional), cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Clean and chop the Zingiber officinale rhizomes into small pieces. Place them in a clean glass jar.
Add 1 cup of carrier oil to the jar with the rhizome pieces. If using vodka, add 3 tablespoons to help extract the oil.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every day to help the oil extract.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and store the essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains the bioactive constituents glabridin and glycyrrhetic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and laxative properties. Glycyrrhetic acid helps to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for easier bowel movements and relieving constipation. Glabridin also has a soothing effect on the gut, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy gut environment. The oils' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce spasms and cramps in the digestive tract, making it easier for stool to pass through. The combination of these properties makes Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils a potential remedy for constipation relief.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for constipation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, 2 cups of water, a saucepan, a strainer, a glass bottle, and a carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil.
Combine the dried roots and water in the saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a glass bottle. Discard the solids.
Let the liquid cool. Add 1-2 tablespoons of the carrier oil and mix well.
Store the essential oil in the glass bottle and use 5-7 drops in a warm bath or mix with a carrier oil for massage to help with constipation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia angustifolia essential oils contains a compound called sennoside A, which is a natural laxative. Sennoside A works by increasing the movement of the muscles in the intestines, helping to move stool through the digestive system. The essential oil also contains anthraquinones, such as rhein and aloe-emodin, which help to soften stool and increase water in the intestines. These properties help to relieve constipation by stimulating bowel movement and making it easier to pass stool. By increasing the movement and softening of stool, Cassia angustifolia essential oils can help to alleviate constipation and promote regular bowel movements.
remedies.image_alt: Cassia angustifolia herbal essential oils for constipation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cassia angustifolia leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat, then add the Cassia angustifolia leaves and let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil) to the liquid and mix well.
Store the Cassia angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and use as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which is responsible for its therapeutic properties. Cinnamaldehyde helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, making it easier for food to pass through and relieving symptoms of constipation. The antispasmodic properties of cinnamaldehyde also help to reduce muscle cramps and spasms in the intestinal walls, further easing the passage of stool. Additionally, cinnamaldehyde has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy bowel function. By relaxing the muscles and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, Cinnamomum verum essential oils may help to alleviate the symptoms of constipation.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal essential oils for constipation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cinnamomum verum (Ceylon cinnamon) bark, 2 cups of water, and a saucepan.
Cut the cinnamon bark into small pieces and add them to the saucepan.
Pour in 2 cups of water and bring the mixture to a boil on medium heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid reduces to 1 cup.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, and let it cool to obtain the Cinnamomum verum essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains menthol, menthone, limonene, and beta-pinene as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have stimulant, antispasmodic, and carminative properties, which help to ease digestive issues and relieve constipation. Menthol, in particular, has a gentle laxative effect, while menthone helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and improve peristalsis. Limonene also has a carminative effect, helping to reduce gas and bloating that can contribute to constipation. By promoting digestive relaxation and ease, Mentha x piperita essential oils may help to alleviate constipation symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for constipation
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water in the saucepan over low heat for 10-15 minutes, then let it cool.
Add the fresh Mentha x piperita leaves to the cooled water and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, then discard the leaves.
Use a hydrosol kit to distill the mixture and collect the essential oil. Store it in a glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rheum officinale essential oils contains bioactive constituents like rhaponticin and rhein. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the digestive system and reduce irritation in the intestines. Rhein, in particular, has been shown to stimulate bowel movements and increase the frequency of defecation. The laxative properties of Rheum officinale essential oils make it a useful natural remedy for constipation. The essential oil's ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract also helps to regulate bowel movements and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedies.image_alt: Rheum officinale herbal essential oils for constipation
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Rheum officinale roots. Clean and dry them thoroughly.
Use a glass jar, add 2 cups of the clean roots and cover them with 4 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Close the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Shake the jar every 2 days.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Rheum officinale essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 5-7 drops per day for constipation relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains several bioactive constituents, including linalool and linalyl acetate. These compounds have a relaxing effect on the digestive system, helping to calm muscle spasms and reduce inflammation in the intestines. Linalool, in particular, has been shown to slow down the movement of food through the digestive system, giving the body more time to absorb nutrients and water. This can help to soften stool and make it easier to pass, alleviating symptoms of constipation. By promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria and reducing inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may help to regulate bowel movements and prevent constipation.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for constipation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia flowers and a clean glass jar.
Combine 1 cup of flowers and 2 cups of water in a pot. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2 hours.
Let the mixture cool. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the glass jar.
Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) to the liquid. Stir well.
Bottle the essential oil mixture and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops as needed to help with constipation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba essential oils contains active constituents like flavonoids and terpenoids, which give it beneficial properties for digestion. These compounds help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and improve blood flow to the intestines, making it easier for food to pass through. The flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, also have anti-inflammatory effects that can soothe irritated tissues in the digestive system. Terpenoids like bilobalide can help regulate bowel movements and prevent constipation by stimulating the muscles in the intestines. By improving digestion and reducing inflammation, Ginkgo biloba essential oils can help alleviate constipation symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal essential oils for constipation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba leaves, a clean glass jar, and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Combine 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil in the glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Discard the solids and store the Ginkgo biloba essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis essential oils contains valerenic acid, isovaleric acid, and valeranone as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have a sedative effect on the muscles in the digestive system, which can help to relax and soothe the intestines. The relaxing properties of valerian root essential oils can help to reduce spasms and cramps that can cause constipation. Additionally, valerian root essential oils have a mild laxative effect due to the presence of valerenic acid, which can help to stimulate bowel movements and relieve constipation. By reducing muscle spasms and stimulating bowel movements, valeriana officinalis essential oils can help to promote regular bowel movements and alleviate constipation.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal essential oils for constipation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Valeriana officinalis roots and flowers. Clean and dry the area where you will be working.
Use a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to create a steamer basket. Place the Valeriana officinalis in the steamer basket.
Fill a large pot with 2 cups of water and bring it to a boil. Reduce the heat and place the steamer basket with the Valeriana officinalis over the water.
Steam the Valeriana officinalis for 2-3 hours. You can monitor the temperature using a thermometer to keep it between 100°F to 150°F.
After steaming, let the Valeriana officinalis cool down. Use a centrifuge or a manual oil press to extract the essential oil. You can expect to get about 2 tablespoons of oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains a blend of biologically active constituents such as bornyl acetate, camphor, and 1,8-cineole. These compounds give the oil its digestive and anti-inflammatory properties. The anti-inflammatory properties of 1,8-cineole can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the gut, which can contribute to constipation. Bornyl acetate has a stimulant effect on the digestive system, helping to increase bowel movements and relieve symptoms of constipation. The antispasmodic properties of camphor can also help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, making it easier for food to pass through.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for constipation
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water in the saucepan over low heat for 30 minutes to release the essential oil from the leaves.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Let the mixture sit for 2 hours to allow the essential oil to separate from the water.
Use a dropper to extract the essential oil from the top layer of the mixture and store it in a dark glass bottle.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea can help prevent constipation. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can relax the muscles in your digestive tract and make it easier to pass stool. Other teas, like senna and dandelion, have natural laxatives that can help stimulate bowel movements. They can also help soften stool.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for constipation every day can be safe if you choose teas made from natural ingredients like senna or peppermint. However, some teas may interact with medications or worsen underlying conditions. Be cautious if you have a weakened digestive system or are prone to stomach issues.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with constipation, but it's not a quick fix. It may take a few days to a week for your body to start feeling the effects. Some teas, like senna or peppermint, can work within 24 to 48 hours, but others, like dandelion or chamomile, may take longer to show results.
faq_time: For constipation, it's best to drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This helps your body digest food from the day's meals and promotes bowel movements. As your body processes food, herbal tea can aid in relaxing the muscles and increasing movement in your intestines.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas like senna and cascara sagrada can affect bowel movements, making constipation worse if you already have it. This is because they stimulate the muscles in the intestines to move food through more quickly. If you're taking prescription medications for constipation, herbal teas with similar effects might interact with them in unpredictable ways.
faq_pregnant: If you're pregnant and have constipation, some herbal teas might help. However, be cautious with certain ingredients. Peppermint tea is often considered safe, as it relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract. Ginger tea might also help, but it's essential to check the tea's quality and ingredients to ensure they're pure and free from contaminants.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's essential to be cautious with herbal teas for constipation. Some herbs, like senna and cascara sagrada, can pass into breast milk and affect your baby. However, peppermint and ginger teas are generally considered safe as they are not absorbed into breast milk in significant amounts. Always check the ingredients and choose a reputable brand.
title_2: Relief from Constipation: 11 Herbal Essential Oils for Older Adults
